Fish: Fish are aquatic animals with a slim, elongated body, fins, and scales. They often display a variety of colors and patterns.
Jellyfish: Jellyfish have a transparent, bell-shaped body with long, trailing tentacles.
Penguin: Penguins have a distinctive black and white countershading, upright posture, and short wings. They may also sometimes have yellow patches near their head.
Puffin: Puffins are small with distinctive brightly colored orange beaks, often standing on rocks outside of the water or swimming on the surface of the water. They also have long waterproof feathers that help them swim and fly.
Shark: Sharks are identifiable by their streamlined bodies and prominent dorsal fins. They are often grey in color.
Starfish: Starfish feature a radial design with several arms emanating from a central disc.
Stingray: Stingrays are flat with diamond-shaped bodies and long, whip-like tails. They are often dark grey in color.
